macro_summary: Stocks edged lower Monday as renewed missile activity near the Strait of Hormuz rattled markets. SPY slipped 0.3% to 718.01; VIX surged 7.7% to 18.29 on the escalation. WTI held at $104.12 after Friday's spike, while US10Y climbed 7bp to 4.45% as oil-driven inflation concerns persisted. Semis were mixed — MU surged 6.8% to a new all-time high on AI demand optimism, while AMD fell 5.6% ahead of tomorrow's earnings and INTC gave back 5% of its recent rally. After the close, Palantir reported Q1 revenue +85% YoY, per Yahoo Finance.
commodities_note: WTI at $104.12 held Monday's spike as Hormuz missile activity continued; analysts warn of $150+ if the strait remains disrupted into Q2 per Bloomberg. Gold eased 0.2% to $4,540. US10Y at 4.45% widens the rate premium over Europe. DXY flat at 98.5.

singapore.summary: The Singapore bank cluster remains under dual-channel NIM compression with US10Y at 4.45% — well above the 4.20% relief threshold — and MAS S$NEER appreciation intact until October 2026. OCBC reports in 3 days as the cluster outperformer (only YoY profit growth) and could break the HOLD stagnation if NIM stabilises.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired — NIM compression persists, OCBC earnings the next catalyst
singapore.key_development: US10Y rose 7bp to 4.45%, widening the rate-driven NIM headwind; OCBC (May 8) and UOB (May 7) earnings this week are the cluster's next inflection points.

semiconductors.summary: All nine semis names are on CAUTION — eight hard-blocked on extreme extension. SOXX slipped 0.6% to $462.06. The cluster is structurally bullish (AI capex, CoWoS expansion, HBM demand) but tactically uninvestable: AMD is +43% above its 50-day, INTC +69%, MU at an all-time high. ASML is the only name below its SMA50, but a distorted R:R (0.3:1 on wide stop) keeps it on CAUTION.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically extended — no clean entry exists
semiconductors.key_development: MU surged to a new ATH (+6.8%) on AI memory demand, while AMD fell 5.6% ahead of tomorrow's earnings. Eight of nine names exceed the 20% extreme extension threshold.

big_tech.summary: BigTech remains extended with AMZN and GOOG both above +20% SMA50. MSFT is the only non-CAUTION name after downgrading to HOLD — post-earnings drift has compressed its R:R to 0.5:1. Palantir reported Q1 revenue +85% after the close; overnight price action will determine whether it graduates from CAUTION. The capex cycle is confirmed but has not yet translated into investable entry points.
big_tech.thesis_status: Structurally confirmed, technically extended — MSFT the only name not on CAUTION
big_tech.key_development: PLTR reported Q1 revenue +85% after Monday's close (per Yahoo Finance); overnight reaction at Tuesday's open is the next signal for the cluster.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp.
interconnected.reason: Received matching $2B NVDA photonics investment alongside LITE — AI datacenter optical interconnect supplier. LITE earnings tomorrow; COHR read-through on photonics demand confirmation.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE's May 6 earnings confirm AI optics revenue acceleration and COHR pulls back 8-10% from current levels on sympathy.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: AI datacenter switching leader — direct beneficiary of hyperscaler $630B 2026 capex cycle. NVDA Blackwell Ultra cluster deployments (Q2-Q3 2026) drive 800G/1.6T switch demand.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after NVDA's May 21 earnings for Blackwell Ultra deployment scale — becomes interesting on a 10-12% pullback from current levels.

interconnected.name: SK Hynix
interconnected.reason: MU's primary HBM competitor — both names surging on AI memory demand. MU hit an ATH Monday; SK Hynix is the read-through for whether HBM pricing is sustainable into 2027.
interconnected.entry_note: Monitor MU's June 25 earnings for HBM bit-shipment guidance; SK Hynix becomes a watchlist candidate if HBM ASPs are guided +30% or higher.

active_risks: Iran-Hormuz Oil Crisis: WTI at $104.12 as missile activity near Strait of Hormuz threatens 20% of global oil transit. US Central Command denied warship strike; UAE detected four missiles. Analysts project $150+ if closure persists into Q2 (per Bloomberg).

US-China Tech Trade War: Section 122 tariffs (15% surcharges, 150 days) and China retaliation with 34% tariffs + export controls continue reshaping semiconductor supply chains. MATCH Act (proposed) would further restrict ASML DUV/EUV exports to China.
Federal Reserve Hawkish Pause: US10Y at 4.45% (+7bp Monday) as oil-driven inflation fears push rate-cut timeline to late 2026. ECB holds steady, widening rate differential.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil above $100 keeps energy costs elevated across supply chains — semis names (ASML, TSM, LITE) face logistics-cost margin pressure. The 4.45% US10Y sustains NIM compression on Singapore banks (DBS, OCBC, UOB) and supports the hawkish floor that caps mega-cap tech multiples. Defense AI spending (PLTR) remains the sole geopolitical beneficiary.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: UAE detected four missiles from Iran near Strait of Hormuz on Monday; US Central Command denied an Iranian strike on a US warship (per Barrons.com, May 4). Trump stated military operations expected to last 4-5 weeks.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: No new entries while 14 of 17 watchlist names are on CAUTION — wait for the May 7 FOMC decision and this week's Singapore bank earnings (OCBC May 8, UOB May 7) to reset the board.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive — no actionable entries across 17 names
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 14 of 17 names are on CAUTION — 11 hard-blocked on extreme extension (>20% above SMA50). The Hormuz oil crisis and 4.45% US10Y sustain structural headwinds for banks and semis alike. FOMC (May 7) and three bank earnings this week are the near-term catalysts that could unlock a signal change.
